text:
The Paris Olympicshave welcomed thousands of athletes from across the world compete on the biggest stage, with many of their close family members supporting from the side lines.

A number of Team GB's athletes are proud parents, from the likes of diver Tom Daley and swimmer Adam Peaty, to silver medallist Amber Rutter and gymnast Max Whitlock.

One thing that hasn't gone unnoticed are their children who have made adorable appearances as their parents make sporting history - and let's be honest it's pretty adorable to watch.

Adam Peaty

Olympic swimmer Adam is the father to his little boy, George, who he shares with ex-partner Eirianedd Munro. George was born in 2020 and was seen cheering on his dad by the side of the pool.

Following his race where he won a silver medal, Adam said: "When I hugged George, as soon as I saw his curly hair, I was gone, I was crying. I feel like I've been stung by a bee because I feel so swollen in my face from crying so much."



READ MORE:Gabby Logan's preparations for Olympics revealed and one thing she 'never' does



Tom Daley

British diver Tom Daley shares two adorable children, Robbie and Phoenix, with his husband. The duo were seen cheering on their dad when he won silver in the men's synchronised 10m platform.

Tom had effectively retired from the sport and wasn't planning to appear in Paris, but was persuaded to return to the Olympics after his eldest son requested to see him dive again.

Amber Rutter

Amber was watched on by her husband and baby Tommy as she clinched silver in the women's skeet final. She gave birth just three months ago and her son was not meant to be in France, apparently.

But, her husband James had secretly flown out with their baby to watch the historic moment.

"I had no idea they were coming,” said Rutter. “I know Tommy might not remember it but I definitely will so I’m so glad they made the journey."

Don't miss:Carol Kirkwood leaves BBC Breakfast fans 'in tears' over announcement

Don't miss:People just realising how rice pudding is made - as Gregg Wallace says 'never'





Max Whitlock

Legendary gymnast Max was reunited with his nearest and dearest following his final appearance for Team GB ahead of retirement.

The 31-year-old gymnast attempted to claim a fourth gold medal in Paris after being inspired to continue by his five-year-old daughter. He sadly missed out on a medal in the pommel horse.

Helen Glover

We use your sign-up to provide content in ways you've consented to and to improve our understanding of you. This may include adverts from us and 3rd parties based on our understanding. You can unsubscribe at any time. Read ourPrivacy Policy

Team GB flagbearer Helen Glover celebrated her Silver medal in the Women’s Coxless Four with her three children - and the scenes were utterly adorable.

Following her appearance on the podium, she was back home with her family in the UK in a matter of days. She lives on the borders of Berkshire and Buckinghamshire with her husband, Steve.
